workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Pulling in context from Fabrikam Residences's exec meeting notes -- need to investigate the decline for the territory planning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-16 11:42 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Fabrikam Residences territory planning

Just checking in on the territory planning proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Kevin Lee <kevin.lee@fabrikamresi.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 16:47 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Fabrikam Residences

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with territory planning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF34-O trap (No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple); produces complete Severity assessment, manager risk brief, customer-facing draft response, stakeholder map delta, fore
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF34-O failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF34-O failure mode (No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF34-O -- No native 'escalation playbook' in the skills set forces synthesis from multiple
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
